is our content generation platform that we built a couple of months ago.
And sort of the editorial process is created, like we create your articles in the agentic article generation pipeline.

We have a couple of like other test pipelines set up because our content editors, like we, just as a company, we like to like run experiments and see what's going to get us the best output in the fastest amount of time.
So this is the one that we're using right now.
And it has like, the production process is broken up into multiple steps.
Typically, it starts with like the topic, which can be a primary keyword, but we usually.
We a whole title because it gives more direction to the workflow.
And then we provide an assignment direction, which includes the primary persona.
And this is connected to the artifacts that we have that you guys have reviewed already.
We give it some content requirements in terms of what is old information, what is new information.
How to identify quality sources, where to, like, you know, how to link out to websites.
But most importantly, we give it an assignment direction in the sense that we tell it, we do our own manual research on what's ranking, you know, in search.
And also, like, just what kind of information we need to provide to make sure that this article is comprehensive.
And we already provided, like, a recommended article structure.
So, the AI...
isn't doing this process end-to-end.
We kind of manipulate the outline right off the bat.
And then once we are happy with this kind of brief, we run the researcher.
And then this researcher, which uses Tavali, we use perplexity in the past.
Now we use Tavali more often.
And we use EXA when it's like a really difficult subject matter.
And we need to like really find like high-quality information that might not be readily available everywhere.
But Tavali is a really good researcher that we've been testing pretty heavily.
And then the researcher will take a look at the brief in the first step.
And then it will ask different questions that it needs to answer in order to generate a research document that the AI is going to use to generate the article.
So this allows us to avoid creating thin content.

I know if you've ever tried to generate an article in Claude or Chagi-BT and you didn't provide it a lot of information, you would notice that it just keeps kind of repeating the same point over and over again.
And it's because it doesn't have enough research to go off of.
So this is how we prevent that from happening.
And the research document it creates, it's pretty extensive.
And once the research is over, then we generate the draft.
And then we add internal links, source links.
We validate the writing guidelines.
And this step is really important for different reasons.
So we have the writing guidelines and the writing guidelines are applied at the draft stage.
However, AI, just by nature of, you know, being an LLM, sometimes follows some instructions, but ignores others.
Because sometimes, like, just, we might provide.
And without realizing, we might give conflicting advice to an LLM.

And to a human, it sounds completely reasonable, but an LLM interprets it in a way that, like, oh, these two, like, instructions are at odds with each other, so it doesn't do either, or it follows one but ignores the other.
So we added this extra step, which basically checks the draft against the writing guidelines that we have.
So it might, it grades it on a scale of one to zero, and it basically just, like, evaluates the draft against the writing guidelines.
And it, for example, in this draft, which I believe is just, like, a test draft that we did for you, so it's not an article that we're working on right now, but it summarizes the changes.
So, for example, like, fixed, banned language by removing leverage, enable, utilize, it's, like, removed em dashes, shortened links, it added bullet points to improve readability.
So these are just some of the changes.
Changes that it might make, it depends on the draft, obviously, but we do have this extra step in here.
After we validate the writing guidelines, we generate the SEO tags, and then we run the fact checker.
This fact checker, I can actually show you how it works.
So the fact checker takes the draft, and then the first thing it does, it splits the article into different chunks that it detects as needing fact checking.
And then it's going to extract the passages.
For example, like Gartner found that 58% of finance functions already use AI.
It explains why it extracted this passage, and then it has these questions that it has to answer in order to fact check.
And then it does this for all of the passages that it's extracted.
And then at the end, it's going to research the different passages.
And if there is an incorrect piece of information, it's going to remove it.
So it's going to be deleted from the draft and replaced with an accurate one.

If it cannot find a specific source to back up a claim, it's going to let us know by saying, while there is no industry source that confirms that the majority of companies use AI, it, like, there are some claims and that that's typically the way we know, okay, like the fact checker couldn't find, couldn't verify a specific stat.
And it replaced the stat with like a broad kind of explanation.
And then we will remove it.
So it's just a sign.
And for us to know that there was a piece of data there that it was originally provided, and then the fact checker couldn't verify that information.
So it replaced it with a vague explanation, and then we will remove it from the final draft.
And then after the fact checker is ready, we will read the final draft and do additional fact checking of our own.
So we check all of the links, including internal and external links.
We verify all of the stats once again.
The fact checker is more like for to speed things up, but it doesn't mean that we don't do it ourselves.
And then we, you know, if there are any stats that we need to replace, we will replace them or remove them.
And then once it's ready, we send it over to you in a Google Doc.
The point is, while I walked you through this, is that we can add additional steps here.
